element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Directional throw control becomes tactical tool for hitting groups

element_introduction_order.element: Rideable beasts (Chicken Legs, Green Dragons, Fire Dragons)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mounted combat changes movement and reach dynamics
Partial meter usage allows graduated power scaling
Gilius: rock magic, Ax Battler: wind magic, Tyris Flare: fire magic
Large enemy groups requiring learned combat patterns
Enemies become more numerous and challenging toward castle

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting magic book to restore power
Mounting beast for temporary invulnerability window
Between-stage story exposition and rating screen
typical_tension_triggers: Entering new stage with fresh enemy waves
Boss encounter with large enemy group
Depleting magic meter in dangerous situation
Mounting beast expires during combat pressure
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based intensity with boss gates; tension builds through stage progression, releases at each area conclusion. Duel mode provides alternate pacing of sustained difficulty without story beats.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Ravaged Village and Ruins establish basic combat patterns and enemy types with moderate threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tower and Dragon's Throat Cave introduce environmental hazards and mixed enemy group compositions
difficulty_curve.late_game: Castle Gates and Castle intensify enemy density; Dark Guld's Chamber requires mastery of all learned mechanics against enhanced opponent
notable_spikes: Stage 1 boss transition from basic enemies to grouped encounter
Stage 7 final boss Dark Guld with Golden Axe enhancement
Duel mode round escalation (15 rounds of increasing difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear seven-stage campaign following narrative arc toward Dark Guld's castle; each stage represents geographic/narrative progression. Between-stage rating awards and exposition reinforce story pacing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stages 1-7: Ravaged Village → Ruins → Tower → Dragon's Throat Cave → Castle Gates → Castle → Dark Guld's Chamber

notable_level_patterns.description: Rideable creatures (Chicken Legs, Green Dragons, Fire Dragons) appear throughout levels as temporary power-ups that change combat reach and enemy interaction patterns, then dismount before boss encounters
Magic books scattered through stages replenish player magic meter; partial meter usage mechanic (hold to charge) allows graduated difficulty management across stage progression
Each of seven stages concludes with large enemy group or boss encounter that must be defeated to progress; acts as skill gate and natural relief point
